figures.caption: Fig. 1: Overview of the control-by-interconnection approach. A GP-dPHS model is used to learn the partially unknown dynamics of the PDE systems. Then, the model enables the design of a boundary controller if the system dissipation dominates the model’s uncertainty.

Fig. 3: Comparison of the evolution of the Hamiltonian with a controller designed based on a model (a) and the actual system dynamics (b). As stated in Proposition 3, the model error leads to a Hamiltonian that converges to a set around the steady state.
